scope: single-GPU S4096 sparse-MLA backward rewrite path after TileLang/rocWMMA route-owned schedules failed the full-boundary latency gate

current_sparse_attention_backward_gate.promotion_rule: A new HIP/CK/rocWMMA cooperative kernel is not a candidate unless q,dO,kv,indices,lse/sink -> dQ+dKV beats the 11.13 ms full sparse-attention backward boundary, not just a late dKV-only reducer slice.

closed_branches.read: Can make detached/final dKV slices faster, but the bridge still materializes P/scaled-dP/selected state before the call, so e2e TFLOPs do not improve.
Global writes shrink, but the route-owned/D-blocked schedule recomputes QK/PV/softmax row terms too expensively and loses query-major reuse.
Validated the same-workgroup score-page mechanism but remains far slower than the 11.13 ms Triton boundary.

new_positive_evidence.validated_surface: CK Tile BF16 M16N16K32 MFMA aliases and a four-wave K16xH64xD32 block shape compile and run on the active MI350 host.

new_positive_evidence.fixed_bug: The previously failed CK contribution-tile probes used the wrong C-fragment row scatter. The passing layout is m=(lane>>4)*4+frag, n=lane&15 for the 16x16 output tile.

new_positive_evidence.read: The mixed rocWMMA score-page plus CK contribution-consumer source-boundary translation unit compiles and runs. S128 is only a build/runtime smoke because it has topk=32, not the S4096 topk=512 target.

Dropping the validated CK contribution tile into the old multi-wave rocWMMA score-page scaffold is slower than the prior rocWMMA contribution consumer and remains far above the full Triton sparse-attention boundary. The useful result is that CK is correct and callable; the old producer/consumer decomposition is rejected as the final schedule.

A direct-source CK score-page producer is slower than the staged rocWMMA score page. It loads q/grad_output/kv directly inside the MFMA loop and loses the shared-staged selected-KV reuse that made the prior score page less bad. This rejects direct-source CK score production as the next schedule.

Replacing both score and contribution MFMA with direct CK in the old source-boundary scaffold regresses further. The decomposition evidence is now strong: CK MFMA arithmetic is correct, but the source-boundary schedule must stage/reuse operands and reduce by route before global memory; a simple CK backend swap is not the path.

next_kernel_contract.name: query_major_ck_mfma_score_page_source_boundary_v1
schedule: Keep CTA ownership query-major: token tile x head block x selected window x D tile.
Compute QK/PV/softmax row terms into a tiny score page in registers/LDS; do not use the rejected direct-source CK score loop.
Consume that score page immediately inside the same workgroup for dQ and CK-MFMA dKV contribution tiles.
Use the validated CK BF16 M16N16K32 contribution tile with store-mode 6 fragment mapping.
Perform group-aware segmented epilogue before global memory; no P/scaled-dP/selected-KV HBM slab is allowed.

first_timing_gates.producer_checksum_source_boundary_ms: must be materially below the rocWMMA score-page producer; target under about 8 ms before adding full dQ+dKV
first_timing_gates.e2e_tflops: do not run or promote e2e TFLOPs until the full sparse-attention boundary beats the retained Triton boundary
